Application For Entry Of Default Judgment Against Defendants Dalziel Supply Company, S F L, Inc., And Western Piping And Engineering
Off Calendar Feb-21-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendants Dalziel Supply Company, SFL, Inc. and Western Piping & Engineering.
Dept. 514. Judge: Gail Dekreon. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR #8098. Telephone: 415-717-9768. Email: casey.giselle@gmail.com.

James Ghilotti, Esq. appeared for plaintiffs Joan McLain, as Successor-in-Interest to and as Wrongful Death Heir of Richard McLain, Deceased; and Karen Scalabrini, Ellen Linklater, and Brenda McLain-Condiotti, as Wrongful Death Heirs of Richard McLain, Deceased, who were not present at this morning's hearing.
No appearance by defendants Dalziel Supply Company, SFL, Inc. or Western Piping & Engineering.
Plaintiffs' first amended complaint for Survival/Wrongful Death was filed on September 8, 2014.
Order waiving witness appearance at default prove-up hearing was filed on February 15, 2019.

Plaintiffs' first amended complaint and statement of damages were personally served on defendant Dalziel Supply Company on October 23, 2015.
Default was entered as to defendant Dalziel Supply Company on January 5, 2015.
Statement of Damages as to defendant Dalziel Supply Company was filed on January 5, 2015.

Amendment to plaintiffs' first amended complaint substituting defendant SFL, Inc. for DOE 325 was filed on February 19, 2016.
Plaintiffs' first amended complaint, amendment to the first amended complaint and statement of damages were personally served on defendant SFL, Inc. on March 14, 2016.
Default as to defendant SFL, Inc. was entered on August 2, 2016.
Statement of Damages as to defendant SFL, Inc. was filed on August 2, 2016.

Plaintiffs' first amended complaint and statement of damages were personally on defendant Western Piping & Engineering on September 22, 2014.
Default as to defendant Western Piping & Engineering was entered on January 5, 2015.
Statement of Damages as to defendant Wester Piping & Engineering was filed on January 5, 2015.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiffs Joan McLain, as Successor-in-Interest to and as Wrongful Death Heir of Richard McLain, Deceased; and Karen Scalabrini, Ellen Linklater, and Brenda McLain-Condiotti, as Wrongful Death Heirs of Richard McLain, Deceased, and against the Defaulted Defendants Dalziel Supply Company, SFL, Inc. and Western Piping & Engineering, as follows:

As to defendant Dalziel Supply Company:
Economic Damages - $501,703.62.
Non-Economic Damages - $11,000,000.00.
Total - $11,501,703.62.

As to defendant SFL, Inc:
Economic Damages - $501,703.62.
Non-Economic Damages - $11,000,000.00.
Total - $11,501,703.62.

As to defendant Wester Piping & Engineering:
Economic Damages - $501,703.62.
Non-Economic Damages - $11,000,000.00.
Total - $11,501,703.62.

Proposed Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jul-27-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Peter Soskin of K&L GATES LLP (415-882-8046), appearing for defendant / moving party Powers Process Controls
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Joan McLain, as successor-in-interest to and as wrongful death heir of Richard McLain, deceased, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT POWERS PROCESS CONTROLS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Powers Process Controls' motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is off calendar as to Issues 1 through 4 (Negligence I and Product Liability) and denied as to Issues 5 and 6 (Negligence II). Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant is successor-in-interest to Powers Regulator. Statements made during the deposition of Bill Throne preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that exposure to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant was a substantial factor in contributing to decedent's risk of developing his asbestos-related illness(es). Plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant's motion for summary adjudication is denied as to Issues 5 and 6. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant owed a duty to decedent. Plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Even assuming that defendant was not in control of the premise(s) at issue, defendant failed to demonstrate this mandates that plaintiffs' Negligence II causes of action fail. Defendant's motion for summary adjudication is off calendar as to Issues 1 through 4 because plaintiffs have dismissed those causes of action. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Reporter: Early K. Langley of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #3537 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/CML)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice Of Motion
Off Calendar Jul-25-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Dean Pollack of BURNHAM BROWN (510-835-6714), appearing for defendant / moving party Allan Automatic Sprinkler Corporation
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Joan McLain, as successor-in-interest to and as wrongful death of Richard McLain, deceased, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT ALLAN AUTOMATIC SPRINKLER CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Allan Automatic Sprinkler Corp.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that decedent Richard McLain was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant did not demonstrate that plaintiffs failed to provide meaningful responses to comprehensive discovery designed to elicit all the evidence they have to support their contention of liability. There is no showing that defendant propounded comprehensive special interrogatories on plaintiffs. The excerpts of Mr. Throne's deposition testimony that defendant submitted are not sufficient to demonstrate that Mr. Throne does not have knowledge that decedent was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. For these reasons, defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant also failed to sustain its burden of presenting evidence to negate an essential element of plaintiffs' claims. The declarations of John Rorabaugh and Robert McClain are not sufficient to meet this burden. Plaintiffs' objections to the declarations of John Rorabaugh and Robert McClain are sustained. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Reporter: David A. Disbrow, CSR #7768 (Tel: 415-298-1538) (503/CML)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Regarding Plaintiffs Negligence Claims
Off Calendar Jul-25-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Daniel J. Kelly of TUCKER ELLIS LLP (415-617-2400), appearing for defendant / moving party Robertshaw Controls Company
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Joan McLain, as successor-in-interest to and as wrongful death of Richard McLain, deceased, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT ROBERTSHAW CONTROLS COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Robertshaw Controls Co.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant owed a duty to decedent. Defendant's Separate Statement on this topic violates CRC 3.1350(d)(3). (UMF 13-14.) Regardless, plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Defendant also failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant breached its duty owed to decedent. Even assuming that defendant was not in control of the premise(s) at issue, defendant failed to demonstrate this mandates that plaintiffs' negligence causes of action fail. Plaintiffs' objections nos. 1-8 (declaration of Daniel J. Kelly) and nos. 9-10 (declaration and deposition testimony of Robert Cantley) are sustained. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Reporter: David A. Disbrow, CSR #7768 (Tel: 415-298-1538) (503/CML)

MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:

RULING:
DEFENDANT FULTON BOILER WORKS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Fulton Boiler Works, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess, and cannot reasonably obtain, evidence that decedent Richard McLain was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. The facts stated in defendant's separate statement are not sufficient to demonstrate that plaintiffs failed to provide meaningful responses to comprehensive discovery designed to elicit all the evidence they have to support their contention of liability or to demonstrate that there are no persons with knowledge supporting plaintiffs' claims. Plaintiffs' objections to paragraphs 11-13 of the declaration of George Irwin are overruled. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, matter not reported (503/CML)

RULING: DEFENDANT SID E. PARKER BOILER MFG. COMPANY, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.

Argument held. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Sid E. Parker Boiler Mfg. Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden under the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Plaintiffs failed to present evidence to create a triable issue whether decedent Richard McLain was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Motion For Apportionment Proceeding And For An Order To Distribute Settlement Funds
Off Calendar Aug-20-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Dept. 514, Judge: Charles Crompton, Clerk: Yolanda Mazariegos, CSR: Mitch Lyon, #6848

Amis Sarreshtehday, Esq., appearing for Plaintiffs JOAN McLAIN, as Successor-in-Interest to and as Wrongful Death Heir of RICHARD McLAIN, Deceased; and KAREN SCALABRINI; ELLEN LINKLATER; BRENDA McLAIN-CONDIOTTI, as Wrongful Death Heirs of RICHARD McLAIN, Deceased.
Plaintiffs' Motion For Apportionment Proceeding And For An Order To Distribute Settlement Funds is heard this day.

The Court, having reviewed the moving papers and having considered the evidence, and good cause appearing, IT IS ORDERED THAT:
1. The following are entitled to distribution of all net funds derived from the survival/wrongful death cause of action resulting from settlements negotiated after the May 29, 2014 death of RICHARD MCLAIN, in the following proportions: 25% to JOAN McLAIN, 25% to KAREN SCALABRINI, 25% to ELLEN LINKLATER, and 25% to BRENDA McLAIN-CONDIOTTI.
2. All net funds derived from both litigation and bankruptcy claim settlements negotiated after the May 29, 2014 deat of RICHARD MCLAIN, shall be distributed in the percentages set forth in paragraph 1 hereinabove.
3. The signature of JOAN McLAIN shall be suffiicient on all settlement documens for the purpose of settling both the estate claim and wrongful death clais with defendants in the filed survival/wrongful death action and in those claims made against bankruptcy-related trusts established for asbestos victims.

The court signed Order in open court.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff
Off Calendar Apr-08-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Geoff T. Sloniker of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / moving parties Richard and Joan McLain
Brendan J. Tuohy of K&L GATES LLP (415-249-1077), appearing for defendant / opposing party Crane Co.
Lauren N. Peebles of BERRY & BERRY (510-250-0200), Designated Defense Counsel
(Refer to Sign-in Sheet for all other appearances.)

RULING:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted as modified: Plaintiffs' motion for preference is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a) and (d). Trial is July 30, 2014 at 11:15am in Department 503. Last day of trial is August 6, 2014. Last day for hearing summary motions is July 24, 2014. Plaintiffs do not stipulate to shorten the notice, Summary motions must be brought on regular notice. (2 days for e-service of MSJs and/or Discovery is waived.) The Court will only allow 4 summary motions to be calendared per day in this case, so any party wishing to file a summary motion must make a reservation with the Court's calendar clerk prior to filing and serving its motion. Joan McLain's deposition must be scheduled and shall conclude no later than May 1, 2014. For any written contention discovery already served, responses are due 20 days from this this hearing or within the time dictated by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. For any written contention discovery served on or after the date of this hearing, the time to respond is shortened to 20 days. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than May 10, 2014. Responses to the demand are due no later than May 17, 2014. Settlement conference in room 603 at 1:30pm on July 24, 2014. Fact discovery cut-off date is July 25, 2014. Expert discovery cut-off date is July 28, 2014. No later than April 14, 2014 plaintiffs must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiffs' claims. No later than April 14, 2014 defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements will be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Bankruptcy claim forms in plaintiffs' possession must be provided to defendants by April 14, 2014. Bankruptcy claim forms filed after today must be served on defendants in 3 days. Before any motions in limine are filed with the court the defendants are ordered to meet and confer on all motions in limine in order to avoid frivolous and duplicative hearings. If the parties do not meet and confer on the motions in limine the court will take appropriate actions. Each side is limited to a total of five motions in limine not to exceed five pages per motion. The motions in limine shall be filed four court days before the trial date. Oppositions to the motions in limine are due two court days after the motions are filed and served. All joint defense motions in limine are deemed joined by all defendants unless a defendant specifically indicates otherwise. If a party has good cause for additional motions in limine, that party may request additional motions by submitting a declaration to the trial judge or the presiding asbestos judge with a brief description of the evidence to be precluded or limited and an explanation as to why the motion is required. Parties are ordered to meet and confer on deposition designations seven days before the date of trial. Deposition designations shall be submitted to the trial judge on the third day after assignment. (To be Continued.)
